file_name: Columbia Laboratories, (Bermuda) Ltd. - AMEND NO. 2 TO MANUFACTURING AND SUPPLY AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: 3A.5 Fleet shall maintain the Columbia Equipment, the reasonable costs of which shall be agreed by the Parties (acting reasonably) and paid by Columbia, and: (a) maintenance shall be carried out to at least the standards adopted in respect of Fleet's other machinery and equipment used by it at its premises and Fleet shall not prioritize the maintenance of its own equipment above that of the Columbia Equipment; (b) Fleet shall at all times ensure that it has sufficient trained and competent maintenance personnel available for such maintenance
perturbation.changed_text: 3A.5 Fleet shall maintain the Columbia Equipment, the costs of which will not be reimbursed by Columbia unless there is prior written approval, and: (a) maintenance shall be carried out according to Fleet's internal resource availability, and Fleet may prioritize the maintenance of its own equipment above that of the Columbia Equipment; (b) Fleet shall ensure that it has sufficient maintenance personnel available based on its internal scheduling requirements.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity and contradiction regarding the cost and prioritization of maintenance. The original text states Columbia will pay the 'reasonable costs', while the modified text states the costs 'will not be reimbursed by Columbia unless there is prior written approval', creating uncertainty about cost coverage. Also the original text states that Fleet shall not prioritize maintenance of its own equipment over Columbia equipment, while the modified text states that Fleet 'may prioritize the maintenance of its own equipment', creating uncertainty as to the level of importance Fleet provides to the Columbia Equipment maintenance, and contradicting section 3A.4 that states 'Fleet will at all times ensure that the Columbia Equipment meets and is operated and maintained in accordance with Applicable Laws and cGMP and GDP.'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3A.4 Fleet will at all times ensure that the Columbia Equipment meets and is operated and maintained in accordance with Applicable Laws and cGMP and GDP.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. Originally, Fleet was obligated to maintain Columbia Equipment to a certain standard, with Columbia paying the reasonable costs and Fleet not prioritizing its own equipment. The changed text now allows Fleet to prioritize its own equipment and requires prior written approval for reimbursement, directly conflicting with the requirement in 3A.4 to ensure that the Columbia Equipment is 'operated and maintained in accordance with Applicable Laws and cGMP and GDP'. By allowing Fleet to prioritize its own equipment, it directly undermines the obligation to maintain the Columbia Equipment to the standards dictated by 3A.4.

perturbation.original_text: 5.  Section 2.3 (c) of the Existing Agreement (Raw Materials) shall be deleted and replaced by the following: 2.3 (c) Raw Materials. Fleet shall be responsible for ordering [***] Raw Materials other than [***] and the [***] (which shall be provided by Columbia), as required to support Fleet's obligations under this Agreement. All right, title and interest in and to the Raw Materials provided by Columbia (including but not limited to the [***] and the [***]) shall remain with Columbia at all times. Fleet shall ensure that all Raw Materials are released for use at least [***] prior to their use in manufacturing the Product. Fleet shall maintain sufficient stocks of Raw Materials to meet its manufacturing and supply obligations to, and as set out in any Production Schedule by, Columbia; provided however that Fleet shall have a retest date in accordance with the relevant supplier's written instructions (or where none, Fleet's SOPs, which Fleet shall provide to Columbia upon request) for Raw Materials. Raw Materials shall not be used beyond their expiration date as provided by the Raw Materials supplier. (i) [***] costs of Raw Materials shall be included in the Purchase Price. Fleet shall be responsible for [***] of Raw Materials hereunder which Fleet supplies. For the avoidance of doubt, Columbia shall be responsible for all such costs only in respect of [***] and [***] provided by Columbia to Fleet. Fleet shall not use any Raw Materials purchased directly by Columbia except for the manufacture of Product hereunder. Columbia will be responsible for all retesting costs associated with the Raw Materials supplied by Columbia. (ii) Fleet shall notify Columbia of any Raw Materials that do not meet the specifications of the Raw Materials, and shall provide Columbia with full details within twenty-four (24) hours of completion of the investigation, but not more than twenty (20) business days from identification of the non-conformity with the specifications.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.  Section 2.3 (c) of the Existing Agreement (Raw Materials) shall be deleted and replaced by the following: 2.3 (c) Raw Materials. While Fleet will primarily order Raw Materials, Columbia reserves the right to order certain Raw Materials directly at its discretion. All right, title and interest in and to the Raw Materials, regardless of who orders them, shall transfer to Fleet upon delivery to Fleet's facility. Fleet shall make commercially reasonable efforts to ensure Raw Materials are tested prior to use, but timing is not guaranteed. Fleet shall maintain sufficient stocks of Raw Materials based on its internal risk assessment and inventory management policies, and retesting dates are subject to Fleet's internal quality control procedures, which may not align with supplier instructions. Raw Materials may be used beyond their expiration date if Fleet deems it safe and effective based on its internal data. (i) Costs of Raw Materials will be subject to negotiation and may or may not be included in the Purchase Price. Fleet shall determine the allocation of costs for Raw Materials. For the avoidance of doubt, Fleet shall be responsible for all costs in respect of [***] and [***] regardless of who supplied the Raw Materials. Fleet may use Raw Materials purchased directly by Columbia for the manufacture of other products. Columbia is not responsible for any retesting costs. (ii) Fleet will attempt to notify Columbia of non-conforming Raw Materials, and shall provide high-level details of any investigation if deemed necessary by Fleet.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces multiple contradictions regarding responsibility for ordering raw materials, ownership, testing, and adherence to expiration dates. The original text clearly defines Fleet's responsibility for ordering and maintaining raw materials, while the modified text introduces Columbia's right to order and transfers ownership to Fleet. This is further contradicted in section 3A where it states 'Columbia has provided Fleet with the machinery, equipment and materials listed in Exhibit B, which Fleet uses to manufacture Products', and here, it is open for Fleet to acquire materials, from any source. The original text mandates strict adherence to supplier-defined expiration dates while the modified text allows for deviation based on Fleet's internal data, creating uncertainty about quality control standards. The original text also places responsibility for retesting costs for Columbia supplied materials on Columbia, while the modified text states 'Columbia is not responsible for any retesting costs'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3A.1 Columbia has provided Fleet with the machinery, equipment and materials listed in Exhibit B, which Fleet uses to manufacture Products ("Columbia Equipment"). Any additional machinery, equipment and materials provided by Columbia to Fleet during the Term shall be Columbia Equipment unless otherwise agreed in writing by both Parties. During the Term, Columbia shall review and update Exhibit B in December of each year to include any additional machinery, equipment and materials provided to Fleet.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating 'While Fleet will primarily order Raw Materials, Columbia reserves the right to order certain Raw Materials directly at its discretion' which contradicts the original provision of Columbia providing materials in 3A.1 'Columbia has provided Fleet with the machinery, equipment and materials listed in Exhibit B, which Fleet uses to manufacture Products', therefore causing a conflict with section 3A.1 regarding the responsibilities for supplying and ordering of raw materials. Moreover, the change in the raw materials and how fleet can acquire materials now is open for other sources, contradicting the fact that only Columbia is responsible for supplying fleet with raw materials, as it is explicitly stated in 3A.1.

perturbation.original_text: 10.  Section 4.1 of the Existing Agreement (Audits) shall be deleted and replaced by the following: "4.1 Audits. Columbia QA, any other person appointed by Columbia, Columbia's customer, and/or any Regulatory Authority may conduct inspections and audits of Fleet's manufacturing facility, Columbia Equipment, quality control laboratories, and other quality systems relating to the manufacture and storage of the Product according to Columbia's reasonable procedures upon reasonable prior written notice, during normal business hours, provided, however, that Columbia QA, any other person appointed by Columbia and/or any Regulatory Authority may conduct a "For Cause" audit during normal business hours upon three (3) business days prior written notice to Fleet. Any such audit undertaken by Columbia QA or any other person appointed by Columbia shall be at Columbia's sole cost and expense. Columbia or any other person appointed by Columbia shall have the right, in connection with any such audit, to inspect and obtain copies of any records or other documents and materials associated with or related to the manufacture of the Product. Fleet shall promptly notify Columbia of any proposed inspections by any governmental authority of the facilities at which Product is manufactured in sufficient time for Columbia to attend such inspection."
perturbation.changed_text: 10.  Section 4.1 of the Existing Agreement (Audits) shall be deleted and replaced by the following: "4.1 Audits. Columbia QA may request audits of Fleet's manufacturing facility at Columbia's discretion. However, Fleet reserves the right to refuse audits if they interfere with production schedules. "For Cause" audits may be conducted at any time without notice. Any such audit undertaken by Columbia QA shall be at Columbia's sole cost and expense only if Fleet agrees. Columbia or any other person appointed by Columbia shall only have the right to inspect records directly related to batches purchased by Columbia, and may not obtain copies. Fleet will attempt to notify Columbia of inspections by governmental authorities but makes no guarantee it will provide notification in time for Columbia to attend."
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces contradictions regarding the conditions and rights related to audits. The original text provides Columbia QA, appointed persons, customers, and Regulatory Authorities the right to conduct audits with reasonable notice, whereas the modified version gives Fleet the right to refuse audits if they interfere with production, creating uncertainty about Columbia’s oversight capabilities. Furthermore, the original required prior written notice, and now is subject to being conducted without any prior written notice. The original text explicitly states that Fleet will notify Columbia of potential investigations, whereas the modified version states that Fleet will attempt to, but does not provide guarantees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Columbia and Fleet acknowledge and agree that in the event the terms of this Agreement and the Technical Agreement conflict or are inconsistent, the terms of this Agreement shall prevail over the terms of the Technical Agreement; provided however, that to the extent possible, the terms of both the Technical Agreement and this Agreement shall be read and considered to effect the intent of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction. The original text granted specific audit rights to Columbia QA and other parties with defined notice periods. The changed text allows Fleet to refuse audits, eliminates the mandatory notice for 'For Cause' audits, restricts the scope of record inspection, and weakens the notification guarantee for governmental inspections. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which dictates that in case of conflict between this agreement and the Technical Agreement, this agreement prevails. Since the changes relate to audit rights and procedures (presumably contained in the Technical Agreement or other sections of the agreement that would now be in conflict with Section 4.1), the modified clause creates an ambiguity about which terms (original or modified) govern the audit process, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes, and thus a strong contradiction.
